title: Normal Intelligence and Emergency Intelligence: From Animal Survival to Autonomous Driving and Future Brain-Unit AI
description: This repository explores the distinction between Normal Intelligence and Emergency Intelligence as a potential architectural principle underlying biological brains, autonomous systems, and future artificial intelligence. Inspired by animal survival behavior, autonomous driving systems, Function Tunnel Networks (FTN), and MET Brain Structure (MBS), the work investigates how routine operation and emergency response may evolve into separate intelligence layers connected through trigger-driven switching mechanisms. The repository discusses evolutionary motivations, autonomous driving implications, AI safety considerations, and possible connections to future Brain-Unit AI architectures. The goal is not to propose a complete implementation, but to highlight a recurring structural pattern that may contribute to safer, more robust, and more evolvable intelligent systems.
